title: Fix host to container port mapping for simple ranges
body:
This fixes the issue that a simple port range should map to a random
port range from the host to the container, if no host port range is
specified. For example this fails without applying the patch:

```
> podman run -it -p 6000-6066 alpine
Error: cannot listen on the TCP port: listen tcp4 :53: bind: address already in use
```

The issue is that only the first port is randomly chosen and all
following in the range start by 0 and increment. This is now fixed by
tracking the ranges and then incrementing the random port if necessary.

Blocks https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/7948

comments.body: So it looks like that the new intended behavior is to choose a random host port if we just specify `-p 80`? I'm wondering why we change this, because in v2.1.1 it behaves like a 1:1 mapping. I'm confused, it looks like that (if the randomness is intended) the port range is broken, since it starts at a random port but continues the range at port `0`…
Should work the same was as Docker.

Podman 1.9 and below would randomly assign if given only a single number,
not a host and container port pair - we broke this in 2.0 and are only
fixing it now.

On Fri, Oct 16, 2020, 06:50 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Should work the same was as Docker.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.

Though, it sounds like the behavior for a range is broken now. I'll verify
what the intended behavior is once I can get in front of a computer.

On Fri, Oct 16, 2020, 08:38 Matthew Heon <matthew.heon@gmail.com> wrote:

> Podman 1.9 and below would randomly assign if given only a single number,
> not a host and container port pair - we broke this in 2.0 and are only
> fixing it now.
>
> On Fri, Oct 16, 2020, 06:50 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Should work the same was as Docker.
>>
>> —
>> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
>> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
>> <https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8043#issuecomment-709973487>,
>> or unsubscribe
>> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCGGEYEISRUSZNUFPODSLAQQTANCNFSM4STB5QPA>
>> .
>>
>
The behaviour in 1.9 appears to be randomly assigning a port for every one in the range. We may want to take ranges without a host port assigned, and split them into individual ports.
All kinds of test unhappiness.  This looks like something that should be documented on any affected man pages too.

The failing use case should now work:
```
> ./bin/podman run --log-level=debug --rm -it -p 8080-8085 alpine 2>&1 | rg "exposing"
time="2020-10-19T11:25:34+02:00" level=debug msg="rootlessport: time=\"2020-10-19T11:25:34+02:00\" level=info msg=\"exposing ports [{41295 8080 tcp } {41296 8081 tcp } {41297 8082 tcp } {41298 8083 tcp } {41299 8084 tcp } {41300 8085 tcp }]\"\n"
```

Didn't think we already had a test to cover this, but it looks like we do.

/approve
LGTM
